title: Chapter 4 – Extermination of the Family (3)
content:
This time, he did not go to the training grounds.

He retreated to his private room where no one was permitted to enter, sat cross-legged, and began to meditate.

This was different from training the body.

Meditation to absorb energy while sitting still required a space free from all outside interference.

'If someone disturbs me and my energy flow gets twisted, I'll die.'

He thought of his family members, who grew recklessly careless whenever it came to him.

It was a worrying thought.

He had no desire to die young with his energy channels tangled into knots.

With a slow breath, he erased the grim image before it could take hold.

Jeong Yeon-shin emptied his thoughts and turned his awareness inward.

He had already grasped the principles of circulating true energy through the small and large meridian channels in his body.

'Let's awaken the power of Jeong Family's martial arts into true energy.'

First, he needed to refine the natural energy he drew in through a specialized breathing technique.

Most martial clans, the Jeong Family Clan and the Zhongnan Sect among them, made the natural energy their own through circulation.

The power that fully adapted to and settled within a practitioner's body was called inner energy, or true energy.

Jeong Yeon-shin wanted to condense his true energy further with true energy.

Just as his body had grown strong by absorbing inner energy.

The training that began at dawn continued until the sun vanished behind the mountains.

"...Huh? This is strange."

Jeong Yeon-shin murmured, his expression dazed. He uncrossed his legs lightly, as though he had only just sat down.

'Why is the Baihui acupoint so wide open?'

At first, he had wanted to test what effect the densified true energy would produce. It hadn't gone well.

The true energy pooled in the dantian seemed to already exist as a single unified mass, refusing to accept another layer.

His approach might have been wrong, yet that didn't explain the overwhelming energy Elder Yeo Il-sin possessed.

Regardless, all of that was now pushed aside.

'I spent an entire year training only my body with Jeong Family's martial arts.'

So consumed had he been in that single pursuit, like a man possessed, that a full year had passed without self-reflection.

The volume of energy coursing through his head had grown even more immense.

'I knew mine was growing abnormally.'

Jeong Yeon-shin muttered, still dazed.

"Am I going to die like this?"

The Baihui acupoint connected directly to the upper dantian in the head, so the energy there should have been proportionally vigorous.

He feared that one day he would simply lose his mind and be found dead.

Comparing the disparity, he estimated less than five years remained before he lost consciousness entirely.

The lifespan he had suddenly calculated felt deeply unreal.

'Die? Me?'

He rose from his seat and gripped the doorknob with trembling hands.

Fresh air might clear his head. It might give him a clue.

Click.

His body froze the instant the door swung open.

Jeong Yeon-shin stood motionless. Riding the night air came the smell of blood.

He seized his sword first. He had watched hunters butcher and debone animals before.

The scent was similar, but no corpse appeared as he left the room and crossed the training grounds.

Such a thing was impossible unless it was the Blood Technique of the Unorthodox Sects, which he had only heard of in stories.

"..."

Silence blanketed the night sky. The residence was unnervingly quiet.

Jeong Yeon-shin walked slowly.

A premonition had already settled over him, dark and certain.

It felt like wading into a pitch-black swamp.

'Ah.'

He reached the courtyard of the main building.

And he saw them. Corpses.

The old saying in the martial world that blood forms a river was an exaggeration.

The blood had either soaked into the ground or trickled down the thresholds of each annex building.

Family members lay scattered and lifeless.

An identical sword wound was carved into every chest, as though someone had been practicing their swordsmanship.

Whether they had been lost in a meditative state and failed to sense the attack, or the enemy's power had simply been too overwhelming for resistance, he could not tell.

Boom!

A tremendous crash rang out from beyond the estate walls.

Formidable masters were clashing outside the main gate.

Amidst the ringing of blades and impacts thick with inner energy, Jeong Yeon-shin walked toward the main building.

About ten figures who appeared to be the attackers sat around in careless repose. Only when the boy drew within ten paces did one of them turn his head.

"Who are you?"

The man wore two swords crossed on his back. Jeong Yeon-shin answered simply.

"The third son of this household."

No reply came. The man rose and walked over with heavy, deliberate steps.

He did not appear to have any intention of drawing his swords.

He closed the distance and brought his hand down in a chopping strike, the blow laced with inner energy.

Jeong Yeon-shin's eyes flashed sky-blue as he read the flow of that energy riding the displaced air.

Pak!

Catch the wrist. Hammer down with the back of the hand. Twist. The sequence flowed naturally, like water finding its course.

A Grappling technique he had never been taught unfolded on its own.

The energy circulation of Jeong Family's martial arts, which strengthened both inner energy and body through motion, surged through him.

His grip was iron. No matter how much force the man applied, the wrist would not come free.

He held it just like that.

Kwak!

A scream tore through the night, accompanied by the sound of thick bone snapping.

Every head turned at once. Eyes widened in unison.

"Let go!"

"You b*st*rd!"

Truly rootless wanderers of the wicked sects, Jeong Yeon-shin thought.

When he obligingly released the wrist, the twin-sword swordsman glared at him.

Pain and fury warred across his face.

It was only natural. He had been subdued by a boy half his size.

The swordsman's large hand clenched into a fist. His face flushed darker than ever, and with a roar, he drove his arm forward.

He seemed to have abandoned all reason, but his momentum was terrifying.

This was no ordinary fist technique. For an instant, his fist appeared to swell as large as a cauldron lid.

Sky-blue lightning flickered through Jeong Yeon-shin's eyes once more.

'The head.'

The trajectory was obvious. The inner energy of Jeong Family's martial arts, already coursing through his body from the Grappling technique, flooded every fiber of his being.

And in that same moment, an insight he had never known before pierced his mind. Intention. It was intention.

When the will to direct inner energy grows fierce enough, the true energy within the body will do anything.

It answers completely to its master.

Woong!

Jeong Yeon-shin's intention took form. The vivid visualization transformed naturally into words, permeating his inner self.

The essence of the martial art, the oral formula, blazed through his body like the sun.

Scripture of Overcoming the Origin.

The name surfaced without reasoning, defining the martial art's identity.

Scripture of Overcoming the Origin. True to its name, it was a circulation method that shattered the practitioner's current limits.

The true energy filling his right foot and left hand rushed to his right hand and left foot.

The overlapping energy doubled in density.

The toes of his left foot cracked the earth with force he had never produced before, and moonlight shivered along the tip of the iron sword drawn in a single flash, tracing the swift sword formula.

Chwaaak--!

The blade caught the necks of the two men charging alongside the twin-sword swordsman.

It felt as though it were someone else's body. Three heads swept from their shoulders at once.

Judging by the shock frozen on their faces, their eyes had managed to follow the sword's speed.

What did it matter? No amount of glaring from heads still tumbling through the air could change anything.

Swish!

Twisting his upper body, he slipped through the gap like a snowflake caught in the wind.

The red-haired woman approaching from behind the fallen men gasped.

By the time she raised her left arm and the crescent-shaped wheel it held, the blue blade had already pierced her throat.

The boy withdrew the sword without hesitation and watched the corpse topple forward.

His breathing was no different from before the killing.

It was an extreme state.

The shock of his first kill had been naturally compartmentalized somewhere in his mind, and his body, tempered by years of martial training, proved steadier and stronger than anyone else present.

"You..."

The thin-bodied swordsman watching from the side twisted his face in disbelief.

The giant man who had rested a hand on the swordsman's shoulder now rose to his feet.

"You've devoted your life to the swift sword. You have no other tricks. You're simply a young and well-trained swift swordsman. Those men let their guard down."

He had the build of a great beast and eyes as cunning as a fox.

Not everything the man said was true. But Jeong Yeon-shin sensed that he had, in fact, exhausted his repertoire.

"Still, it's remarkable. Truly remarkable. How can someone his age do that?"

"Blood Flame Sect?"

The boy asked, glancing at the red-haired woman crumpled at his feet.

His gaze never left the man.

"No. I am from the Blade Sect."

"You're from the Blade Sect? This is clearly the Blood Flame Sect's work."

"That's right."

"What are two of the Thirteen Demons doing in a backwater like Xinye County?"

"That's none of your concern."

"Then why do you keep answering my questions?"

"Because it's a waste. A martial artist trained this well at your age is rare. And it's amusing, watching a child raised in the countryside speak as though he's walked the heart of the martial world."

The man laughed, rough and low.

"A waste?"

A sneer curled across the boy's lips.

"You're just afraid of my maternal family. You rootless bastard of the wicked sects."

Jeong Yeon-shin wagered everything on the bluff, wearing an expression of supreme arrogance while his heart hammered against his ribs.

If the man held back, Jeong Yeon-shin would win.

He had formed his guess the moment the twin-sword swordsman struck with his bare hand rather than drawing a blade.

"...You crazy little brat."

The man growled. The sound was a beast's snarl dragged up from somewhere very deep.

Combined with the ferocious pressure radiating from him, it was enough to raise every hair on end, but Jeong Yeon-shin fought to keep his composure.

"You b*st*rds can't kill me. No matter what I do, the result is the same."

The one who flinches first will be devoured.

Bang!

The sound erupted from the main gate. Shattered door panels flew in every direction with the blast.

The figure rolling amid the wreckage was familiar.

It was the master of the Zhongnan Sect who had accompanied Yeo Il-sin.

His once-immaculate martial arts uniform was torn in several places, and blood trailed from the corner of his mouth.

'His title was Precipice Sword. Even a master of his caliber is in that state...?'

Thud! Thud!

The startled boy turned his head.

The sound of collisions, as though titanic forces were crashing together, drew steadily closer. The combatants burst through the estate fence.

Kwang!

Yeo Il-sin and another Zhongnan Sect master came first.

They were battered beyond anything the Jeong family could have imagined. No better off than Precipice Sword.

The sight of Yeo Il-sin's left ear severed above the lobe, something that seemed almost inhuman, was truly shocking.

'I thought she was a sword immortal who roamed the heavens.'

Thump.

A massive shadow strode forward.

It was unmistakably a human silhouette.

The crushing wave of energy it projected, as though declaring this was the center of the world, felt surreal.

He carried a great sword, and from its edge, an aura that whispered of death rippled outward.

Merely facing him sent ice down the spine.

It was not just the energy. It was not simply the level of martial arts.

He was wreathed in something beyond power, something that defied comprehension.

Jeong Yeon-shin was reminded of an avalanche that nothing could stop.

'Who the hell is he?'

Terrifying energy writhed around the middle-aged man like a living thing.

The absolute swordsman's sword energy.

His gaze swept past the man who had been confronting Jeong Yeon-shin, then settled on the boy. The very air seemed to erode beneath the weight of it.

Simply realizing those eyes had found him made Jeong Yeon-shin dizzy.

"Sect Leader!"

The man who had been speaking with Jeong Yeon-shin threw himself prostrate.

'Sect Leader? The leader of the Blade Sect has come in person?'

The one who commands a pillar of the Thirteen Demons, a force that rivals the Nine Sects.

Then the presence made sense.

The authority that could halt everything around him. The fact that the Zhongnan Sect elders had been wounded even fighting together.

If anything, the Zhongnan masters who had traded blows with such a being for so long were the ones who deserved awe.

In that frozen moment, when everyone had stopped.

Tap, tap.

Jeong Yeon-shin alone moved. He broke into a sprint.

He drove his legs into the ground, pushing Jeong Family's martial arts to its absolute limit.

Toward the man prostrating before the Blade Sect's leader. The terrifying warrior against whom he had just waged a battle of nerves.

"Wha-- what."

The man flinched, and every gaze in the courtyard locked onto the boy.

The leader of the Blade Sect did not move.

Was he waiting to see what would happen? Jeong Yeon-shin didn't care. He threw himself forward with everything he had.

Saaa--!

The air screaming past his ears sounded like a waterfall.

A razor line of sword light traced his sprint.

Thud!

The blue blade punched through the back of the man who was scrambling to rise.

With a wet impact, the man, skewered from spine to belly, collapsed once more.

Jeong Yeon-shin straightened slowly.

One foot planted on the man's back, he stared directly at the leader of the Blade Sect.

"Even now."

A faint jade-colored lightning flickered in the boy's eyes.

"Can you kill me?"
